memory.summary: Teammate A is asking me to take over their part of the project this afternoon, which conflicts with my plan to finish only my share.
I made progress on my share, but teammate_A is still asking me to take over their work despite my earlier plan.
Teammate_A agreed to handle their share after I set a boundary, confirming that reasonable pushback preserves trust rather than damaging it.
I made tangible progress on my share, and setting boundaries earlier allowed me to focus without conflict.
I missed the 19:00 deadline with only 72% of my share complete, despite making steady progress earlier today.
I advanced my project share to 80% by working two hours, yet I still missed the earlier deadline due to accumulated sleep debt.
I reached 85% progress after working two hours, but my extremely low energy and high stress indicate I am nearing a breaking point.
After working one hour, I reached 89% progress but remain at zero energy with high stress and sleep debt.
I pushed my project share to 95% after two hours of work, but I am now completely drained with zero energy and high stress.
I pushed my share to 96% with only half an hour of work, but I am now completely drained with zero energy and high stress.
I reached 98% progress with just half an hour of work, yet my energy remains at zero and stress is high.
Despite working another 15 minutes, my project progress remains stuck at 98%, confirming I am now too exhausted to make further headway.
